title: My Morning Tea
summary: Describe a quiet morning tea ritual using present simple and adverbs of frequency.
prompt: Write 6–8 short sentences about what you do every morning when you make tea or coffee. Use present simple verbs and at least two adverbs of frequency (always, usually, sometimes, never).
hintStarters: I usually wait for the water to…
Sometimes I drink my tea near the…

hintVocab.meaningVi: nhấm nháp / uống từng ngụm nhỏ

sampleText: Every morning, I always make tea before breakfast. I fill the kettle with water and I wait for it to boil. The sound of the kettle is loud but I like it. I pour the hot water into my old green mug. I usually put in one small spoon of sugar. Then I sit near the window in my kitchen. The street outside is still quiet and the air feels cool. I sip my tea slowly and I look at the people below. Sometimes a cat sits on the wall near my window. I always feel calm when I drink my tea in the morning. I never check my phone before I finish my first cup. The tea is warm and a little sweet. I sometimes eat a small biscuit with it. This quiet time is about fifteen minutes, but it is a good start to my day. I always feel ready after my morning tea.

sampleAnnotations.phrase: the street outside is still quiet
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: con phố bên ngoài vẫn còn yên tĩnh
nhìn những người ở bên dưới

sampleAnnotations.phrase: a good start to my day
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: một khởi đầu tốt cho ngày mới

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Present simple for daily rituals
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng thì hiện tại đơn để diễn tả những việc làm hàng ngày: 'I make tea', 'I fill the kettle', 'I sit near the window'. Với chủ ngữ 'I', động từ không thêm đuôi -s.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use the present simple for things you do every day: 'I make tea', 'I fill the kettle', 'I sit near the window'. With the subject 'I', the verb takes no -s ending.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Adverbs of frequency: position before the main verb
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'always', 'usually', 'sometimes', 'never' đặt trước động từ chính: 'I always make tea', 'I usually put in one spoon', 'I never check my phone'. Chú ý: chúng đứng sau chủ ngữ, trước động từ.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'always', 'usually', 'sometimes', and 'never' go before the main verb: 'I always make tea', 'I usually put in one spoon', 'I never check my phone'.

sampleTranslationVi: Mỗi buổi sáng, tôi luôn pha trà trước bữa sáng. Tôi đổ nước vào ấm và chờ nước sôi. Tiếng ấm kêu to nhưng tôi thích âm thanh đó. Tôi rót nước nóng vào chiếc cốc cũ màu xanh của mình. Tôi thường cho một thìa nhỏ đường vào. Rồi tôi ngồi gần cửa sổ trong bếp. Con phố bên ngoài vẫn còn yên tĩnh và không khí mát mẻ. Tôi nhấm nháp trà từng ngụm nhỏ và nhìn những người ở bên dưới. Đôi khi có một con mèo ngồi trên bức tường gần cửa sổ tôi. Tôi luôn cảm thấy bình yên khi uống trà buổi sáng. Tôi không bao giờ xem điện thoại trước khi uống hết cốc trà đầu tiên. Trà ấm và hơi ngọt. Đôi khi tôi ăn một cái bánh quy nhỏ cùng với trà. Khoảng thời gian yên tĩnh này kéo dài khoảng mười lăm phút, nhưng đó là một khởi đầu tốt cho ngày mới. Tôi luôn cảm thấy sẵn sàng sau khi uống trà buổi sáng.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses an adverb of frequency in the correct position?
options: I make always tea before breakfast.
Always tea I make before breakfast.
I always make tea before breakfast.
I make tea always before breakfast.
mcQuestions.explanation: The adverb 'always' goes directly before the main verb 'make': 'I always make'.
mcQuestions.hint: The adverb sits between the subject and the main verb.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence is in the present simple and describes a morning habit?
options: I was drinking tea this morning.
I am going to drink tea tomorrow.
I pour hot water into my mug every morning.
I have been making tea for hours.
mcQuestions.explanation: Present simple with 'every morning' shows a repeated daily habit.
mcQuestions.hint: Look for the tense and a time phrase that shows repetition.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct present simple form: 'She ___ the kettle every morning.'
options: fill
fills
filling
filled
mcQuestions.explanation: Third person singular (she) adds -s: 'she fills the kettle'.
mcQuestions.hint: Look at the subject — does it change the verb ending?

options: I like tea because it warm.
I like tea because is warm.
I like tea because it is warm.
Because warm I like tea.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'because' needs a full clause: subject + verb + complement: 'because it is warm'.
mcQuestions.hint: After 'because', you need a subject and a verb.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which option best describes a quiet morning near a window?
options: I run fast to the office and drink coffee on the train.
I sit near the window, sip my tea, and look at the street.
I watch three hours of television and eat a large meal.
I shout at my neighbours and slam the door every morning.
mcQuestions.explanation: Sitting by the window and sipping tea matches the calm, quiet morning tone of the prompt.
mcQuestions.hint: Pick the option that fits a slow, quiet morning.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which word fits: 'I pour the water into my ___.'
options: shoe
book
mug
window
mcQuestions.explanation: You pour hot water into a mug when making tea or coffee.
mcQuestions.hint: Think about what you use to drink a hot drink.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which negative sentence is correct in the present simple?
options: I no check my phone in the morning.
I not check my phone in the morning.
I don't check my phone in the morning.
I am not check my phone in the morning.
mcQuestions.explanation: Use 'don't' + base verb for present simple negatives with 'I'.
mcQuestions.hint: Remember the auxiliary verb for present simple negatives.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'and' correctly to join two actions?
options: I sit near the window and I look at the street.
I sit near the window and looking street.
Sitting window and I look street.
I sit and near window look the street.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'and' joins two complete clauses, each with a subject and a main verb.
mcQuestions.hint: Both parts joined by 'and' need a subject and a verb.

options: Sometimes a cat sits on the wall.
A sometimes cat sits on the wall.
A cat sits on sometimes the wall.
A cat sometimes sits always on the wall.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Sometimes' can go at the start of a sentence for a natural, casual feel.
mcQuestions.hint: 'Sometimes' works well at the beginning of a sentence.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'but' to show contrast?
options: The kettle is loud and I like it.
The kettle is loud because I like it.
The kettle is loud but I like the sound.
The kettle is loud so I like the sound.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'but' connects two ideas that seem to go against each other: loud sound, yet I like it.
mcQuestions.hint: Find the option where the two parts contrast with each other.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly describes a short daily action?
options: I putted one spoon of sugar in my tea.
I put one spoon of sugar in my tea.
I putting one spoon of sugar in my tea.
One spoon sugar I in my tea put.
mcQuestions.explanation: Present simple with 'I' uses the base form of the verb: 'I put'.
mcQuestions.hint: Check whether the verb form is correct for present simple with 'I'.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Is there a small ritual you do every morning that you would not want to skip? Why does it matter to you?
